name: General Motors Corp.
summary:
General Motors (GM) reported earnings of $3.57 per share on revenue of $48.03 billion for the second quarter ended June 2026. The consensus earnings estimate was $3.13 per share on revenue of $46.65 billion. The Earnings Whisper number was $3.30 per share. The company beat expectations by 8.18% while revenue grew 1.92% on a year-over-year basis.

The company said it expects 2026 earnings of $12.00 to $14.00 per share. The company's previous guidance was earnings of $11.50 to $13.50 per share, and the current consensus earnings estimate is $12.88 per share for the year ending December 31, 2026.

Mary Barra, CEO, said, "The business continues to perform very well. Customer demand in North America remains strong driven by our very attractive lineup of pickups and SUVs. Pricing is consistent, and we delivered the best quarter and first half ever for new Super Cruise-equipped vehicles."

General Motors Company designs, builds and sells cars, trucks and automobile parts. The Company also provides automotive financing services through General Motors Financial Company, Inc.
subject: General Motors Beat Expectations

name: 3M Company
summary:
3M (MMM) reported earnings of $2.40 per share on revenue of $6.50 billion for the second quarter ended June 2026. The consensus earnings estimate was $2.27 per share on revenue of $6.40 billion. The Earnings Whisper number was $2.31 per share. The company beat expectations by 3.90% while revenue grew 2.46% on a year-over-year basis.

The company said it expects 2026 earnings of $8.80 to $8.95 per share with revenue of more than $26.07 billion. The company's previous guidance was earnings of $8.50 to $8.70 per share on revenue of approximately $25.90 billion, and the current consensus earnings estimate is $8.74 per share on revenue of $25.07 billion for the year ending December 31, 2026.

"We delivered a strong second quarter, exceeding expectations with mid-single-digit sales growth, robust operating margins of about 25%, and double-digit EPS growth, reflecting the progress we're making on our strategic priorities and building a higher-performing company," said William Brown, 3M Chairman and CEO. "As a result of our strong first-half performance and continued momentum, we are increasing our full-year guidance and remain confident in our ability to create long-term value for shareholders. I want to thank the 3M team for their disciplined execution, dedication, and relentless focus on delivering innovative solutions for our customers."

3M believes science helps create a brighter world for everyone.
subject: 3M Beat Expectations
